source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The figure on the left consists of two rectangles. Two side lengths are marked: 11 and 13. The figure is cut into three parts along the two lines drawn inside. These can be put together to make the triangle shown on the right. How long is the side marked $\mathrm{x}$?
<image1>

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to visual layout: 'figure on the left', 'triangle shown on the right', and 'two lines drawn inside'
The needed geometric relationships are not fully specified in text alone; the marked side x depends on the diagram and rearrangement
multi_step: Solving likely requires combining at least two ideas: using the given side lengths 11 and 13 and tracking how pieces are cut/reassembled into the triangle
This is an open-response geometry transformation problem rather than a direct lookup or single-step computation
The prompt describes a composite figure, partitioning into three parts, and recomposition into a new shape, indicating multi-stage reasoning
verifiability: answer_type is numeric/number and answer_text is a single concrete value: 37
registry_passed=true, with question, answer, and image assets present
The target is a clearly defined side length x, which is amenable to rule-based or solution-based checking

decision_reasons: question, answer, and required image assets are all present
asset registry passed with no reported issues
high verifiability because the answer is a single numeric value
strong image dependency is acceptable here because the image exists and is available for reasoning
multi-step geometry reasoning value is reasonably high, making it a good candidate
